scope: Phase 5z Wave 1 / 1b / 3 — Round 3 focused polish verification of fig_ew_transition_phase_diagram

structural_checks_cross_ref.note: Round 3 focused only on fig_ew_transition_phase_diagram. Other figures' verdicts unchanged from Round 2.

figures.name: fig_bhl_bilocal_correction
figures.notes: Round 2 verification: master title 'Phase 5z Wave 1b — BHL gap problem and Hill 2025 bilocal correction' now sits cleanly above subplot titles (margin t=110 took effect). Left-panel y-axis is bounded [50, 400] GeV — the spurious 10^126 autoscale is gone. BHL-benchmark 311 GeV label sits next to the curve top without colliding with the PDG line. Hill-recovery dashed line at 125 GeV with the 'Hill recovery (dilution ≈0.403)' tag is unambiguous. Right panel: 'm_t = 220 (BHL)' annotation is clear of the title; 'm_t PDG = 172.57' label sits at the bottom of the panel and no longer collides with x-tick '200'. All four flagged Round-1 issues resolved.

figures.name: fig_ew_transition_phase_diagram

issues: Right-panel y-range extended to [-0.05, 1.0] (verified at src/core/visualizations.py:7684) — the E_threshold = 0.005 horizontal blue line and the 'E = 0.005 (crossover boundary)' annotation now sit cleanly inside the panel rather than clipping the bottom edge. SM star at (125.20, 0.01) and threshold line are both clearly visible.

Cosmetic nit (non-blocking): the 'CFH 1999 endpoint m_H = 72 GeV' annotation at top-left of panel 2 visually butts up against the right-edge legend; text remains fully legible — does not warrant another polish pass.

Left panel: broken-phase wells at φ ≈ ±174 GeV (= 246/√2) fully visible with ±300 GeV x-range; T=0 broken, T=T_c/2 (≈70 GeV), T=1.5T_c symmetric curves render with correct qualitative ordering. T_c ≈ 139.1 GeV.

Right panel: amber first-order shaded region + explicit blue E=0.005 crossover-boundary line both visible inside the panel; SM star at (125.20, 0.01) sits just above the boundary as expected; CFH 1999 vertical dashed reference at m_H = 72 GeV correctly placed.

Citation correction confirmed: KLRS 1996 → CFH 1999 (Csikor-Fodor-Heitger) attribution for the 72 GeV refined endpoint matches paper 22's corrected attribution (annotation source visualizations.py:7677).

Legend now includes the amber 'first-order region (E > threshold)' entry alongside the three V_T(φ) traces (verified at src/core/visualizations.py:7651-7652 with showlegend=True). Round-2 missing-legend-entry recommendation resolved.
Color palette consistent with project conventions (amber, steel_blue, sage cross). Master title + subtitles cleanly separated.

figures.notes: Round-3 verification: both Round-2 polish recommendations have landed cleanly in src/core/visualizations.py:fig_ew_transition_phase_diagram — (1) y-range extended to [-0.05, 1.0] so E_threshold line sits inside the panel; (2) legend entry added for the amber first-order region. KLRS→CFH 1999 citation correction also confirmed in figure annotation. Status promoted WARNING → PASS. Figure is publication-ready for paper 22.

figures.name: fig_higgs_mass_parameter_scan

issues: Master title 'Phase 5z Wave 1 — microscopic m_H prediction over (Λ_UV, G_c)' and the Fiducial subline still sit very close to the top of the heatmap; the subline 'Fiducial N_f=15, λ_4=0.13; gold contour = 125.25 GeV target ± 50% (Lean: scalar_rung_quantitative_EWSB_iff_m_H_matches)' touches the top edge of the plot area. Title margin should be increased by ~30 px to fully clear the heatmap edge.

X-axis tick text now renders as '10^0, 10^5, 10^4, 10^-2, 10^-4, 10^-7' which is monotonically wrong — the labels are out of physical order on a Λ_UV axis that physically runs 1 GeV → 10^17 GeV. The Round-1 superscript-clipping diagnosis (kaleido font fallback) appears to remain. Recommend `type='log'` axis OR ASCII `10^N` ticktext OR LaTeX-style `\text{10}^{17}`.
Title/subline crowding at top of plot.
Tick label glyph rendering still problematic on x-axis.

figures.notes: User flagged this figure as low-priority WARNING and explicitly out-of-scope for the Round-2 fix pass. Heatmap, gold ±50% contour band, and viridis colorbar render correctly; physics is unchanged. Carrying forward as WARNING (status = warning, blocking = false).

summary: Round 2: 1 of 2 FAIL → PASS (fig_bhl_bilocal_correction). 1 of 2 FAIL → WARNING (fig_ew_transition_phase_diagram — both blocking issues fixed; minor annotation overlap remains). fig_higgs_mass_parameter_scan unchanged WARNING (out-of-scope this round). No physics-correctness regressions introduced by the fixes; in fact the EW phase-diagram broken-phase wells are now physically visible for the first time. | Round 3 (2026-04-25): fig_ew_transition_phase_diagram promoted WARNING→PASS after both Round-2 polish recommendations landed (right-panel y-range [-0.05, 1.0] making the E_threshold line visible inside the panel + legend entry for amber first-order region) plus citation correction KLRS 1996 → CFH 1999. 2/3 figures now PASS; only fig_higgs_mass_parameter_scan remains WARNING (deferred / out-of-scope).
recommendations: Optional (deferred): for fig_higgs_mass_parameter_scan, switch x-axis to plotly `type='log'` native ticks to fix the kaleido superscript clipping; bump margin=dict(t=130) to clear the subline from the heatmap edge.
round_2_verdict: Both Round-1 FAIL figures are now non-blocking. fig_bhl_bilocal_correction is fully clean. fig_ew_transition_phase_diagram has minor cosmetic friction but is publishable. The Higgs-mass scan WARNING remains as previously scoped.
round_3_verdict: fig_ew_transition_phase_diagram: PASS. Both Round-2 polish recommendations (y-range [-0.05, 1.0] for visible E_threshold line + legend entry for amber first-order region) landed cleanly in src/core/visualizations.py. KLRS 1996 → CFH 1999 citation correction also confirmed in figure annotation. Figure is publication-ready for paper 22. fig_higgs_mass_parameter_scan unchanged (deferred). fig_bhl_bilocal_correction unchanged PASS.
